requirements.full_sentence: (b) Banks shall ensure that all their branches provide facility for exchange of notes and coins not only to their customers but also others.
requirements.object: branches provide facility for exchange of notes and coins not only to their customers but also others

requirements.full_sentence: Small Finance Banks (up to two years from the commencement of their banking business) and Payment Banks may exchange mutilated and imperfect notes at their option.
requirements.object: notes at their option

requirements.full_sentence: (c) Considering that handling coins packed in sachets of 100 pieces each or small value-based sachets would be more convenient for the cashiers as well as the customers, such sachets shall be kept at the counters and made available to the customers.
requirements.object: counters and made available to the customers

requirements.full_sentence: (d) All branches shall provide the above facilities to members of public without any discrimination on all working days.
requirements.object: to members of public without any discrimination on all working days

requirements.full_sentence: (e) The availability of the above-mentioned facilities at the bank branches shall be given wide publicity for information of the public at large.
requirements.object: for information of the public at large

requirements.full_sentence: (f) None of the bank branches shall refuse to accept small denomination notes and / or coins tendered at their counters.
requirements.object: denomination notes and / or coins tendered at their counters

requirements.full_sentence:
However, with a view to mitigating hardship to the public in genuine cases, it has been provided that the RBI may, with prior sanction of the Central Government, prescribe
<image>
the circumstances in, and the conditions and limitations subject to which, the value of such currency notes or banknotes may be refunded as a matter of grace.
requirements.object: prior sanction of the Central Government, prescribe <image> the circumstances in, and the conditions and limitations subject to whic

requirements.full_sentence: A 'soiled note' means a note which has become dirty due to normal wear and tear and also includes a two piece note pasted together wherein both the pieces presented belong to the same note and form the entire note with no essential feature missing.
requirements.object: become dirty due to normal wear and tear and also includes a two piece note pasted together wherein both the pieces presented belong

requirements.full_sentence: These notes shall be accepted over bank counters in payment of Government dues and for credit to accounts of the public maintained with banks.
requirements.object: counters in payment of Government dues and for credit to accounts of the public maintained with banks

requirements.full_sentence: However, in no case, these notes shall be issued to the public as re-issuable notes, and such notes shall be deposited in currency chests for onward transmission to RBI offices as soiled note remittances for further processing.
requirements.object: public as re-issuable notes, and such notes shall be deposited in currency chests for onward transmission to RBI offices as soiled no

requirements.full_sentence: Mutilated notes shall be presented at any of the bank branches.
requirements.object: of the bank branches

requirements.full_sentence: The notes so presented shall be accepted, exchanged and adjudicated in accordance with NRR, 2009.
requirements.object: adjudicated in accordance with NRR, 2009

requirements.full_sentence: The branches shall take steps to see that the exchange facilities are not cornered by private money changers or professional dealers of defective notes.
requirements.object: that the exchange facilities are not cornered by private money changers or professional dealers of defective notes

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) Imperfect Notes
Imperfect note means any note, which is wholly or partially, obliterated, shrunk, washed, altered or indecipherable but does not include a mutilated note.
requirements.object: wholly or partially, obliterated, shrunk, washed, altered or indecipherable but does not include a mutilated note

requirements.full_sentence: Imperfect notes which can withstand normal handling shall be presented at any of the bank branches for exchange.
requirements.object: of the bank branches for exchange

requirements.full_sentence:
Notes which cannot withstand Normal Handling
Notes which have turned extremely brittle, charred or inseparably stuck up together and, therefore, cannot withstand normal handling, shall not be accepted by the bank branches for exchange.
requirements.object: bank branches for exchange

requirements.full_sentence: Instead, the holders shall be advised to tender these notes to any of the Issue Office of Reserve Bank of India located at Ahmedabad, Bangalore, Belapur (Navi Mumbai), Bhopal, Bhubaneswar, Chandigarh, Chennai, Guwahati, Hyderabad, Jaipur, Jammu, Kanpur, Kolkata, Lucknow, Mumbai, Nagpur, New Delhi,
requirements.object: these notes to any of the Issue Office of Reserve Bank of India located at Ahmedabad, Bangalore, Belapur (Navi Mumbai), Bhopal, B

conditions: Where the number of notes presented by a person is up to 20 pieces with a maximum value of ₹5000 per day
requirements.full_sentence: 6.1.1 Notes presented in small number: Where the number of notes presented by a person is up to 20 pieces with a maximum value of ₹5000 per day, banks shall exchange them over the counter, free of charge.
requirements.object: counter, free of charge

conditions: Where the number of notes presented by a person exceeds 20 pieces or ₹5
requirements.full_sentence: 6.1.2 Notes presented in bulk: Where the number of notes presented by a person exceeds 20 pieces or ₹5,000 in value per day, banks shall accept them against receipt, for value to be credited later.
requirements.object: for value to be credited later

requirements.full_sentence: The probable date of payment shall be informed to the tenderers on the receipt itself and the same shall not exceed 7 days.
Bank account details shall be obtained from the tenderers for crediting the exchange value by electronic means.
requirements.object: tenderers for crediting the exchange value by electronic means

requirements.full_sentence: 6.2.1 All bank branches shall exchange mutilated and imperfect notes and issue receipt for the notes presented for adjudication as per procedure laid down in Part III of NRR, 2009 (www.rbi.org.in→Publications→Occassional) and follow the guidelines mentioned in the following paragraphs, 6.2.2, 6.2.3 and 6.2.4 of this Master Direction, for notes presented in small numbers and in bulk respectively.
requirements.object: notes and issue receipt for the notes presented for adjudication as per procedure laid down in Part III of NRR, 2009

conditions: Where the number of notes presented by a person is up to 10 pieces
requirements.full_sentence: 6.2.2 Notes presented in small number: Where the number of notes presented by a person is up to 10 pieces, bank branches shall normally adjudicate the notes as per the procedure laid down in Part III of NRR, 2009 and pay the exchange value over the counter.
requirements.object: as per the procedure laid down in Part III of NRR, 2009 and pay the exchange value over the counter

conditions: Where the number of notes presented by a person exceeds 10 pieces or ₹5
requirements.full_sentence: 6.2.3 Notes presented in bulk: Where the number of notes presented by a person exceeds 10 pieces or ₹5,000 in value per day, bank branches shall accept them against receipt, for value to be credited later.
requirements.object: for value to be credited later

conditions: If the non-chest branches are not able to adjudicate the mutilated notes
requirements.full_sentence: 6.2.4 If the non-chest branches are not able to adjudicate the mutilated notes, the notes shall be received against a receipt and sent to the linked currency chest branch for adjudication.
requirements.object: receipt and sent to the linked currency chest branch for adjudication

requirements.full_sentence: The probable date of payment shall be informed to the tenderers on the receipt itself and the same shall not exceed 30 days.
6.3 Banks shall levy service charges as permitted in Master Circular on Customer Service in Banks (DBR.No.Leg.BC.21/09.07.006/2015-16 dated July 1, 2015).
requirements.object: permitted in Master Circular on Customer Service in Banks (DBR

conditions: In case of grievance of a tenderer against a specific bank
requirements.full_sentence:
Grievance Redressal
In case of grievance of a tenderer against a specific bank, a complaint may be lodged with the bank branch concerned.
requirements.object: bank branch concerned

requirements.full_sentence: the Branch Manager and every Officer- in-charge of the Accounts or Cash Wing of the Branch shall act as 'Prescribed Officer' in each branch to adjudicate the notes received at the branch for exchange in accordance with NRR, 2009.
requirements.object: in each branch to adjudicate the notes received at the branch for exchange in accordance with NRR, 2009

requirements.full_sentence: After adjudicating mutilated notes, the Prescribed Officer is required to record his order by subscribing his initials to the dated 'PAY'/ 'PAID'/ 'REJECT' stamp.
requirements.object: by subscribing his initials to the dated 'PAY'/ 'PAID'/ 'REJECT' stamp

requirements.full_sentence: The 'PAY' /'PAID' & 'REJECT' stamps shall also carry the name of the bank and branch concerned and such stamps held under the custody of the 'Prescribed Officer' to avoid misuse.
requirements.object: of the bank and branch concerned and such stamps held under the custody of the 'Prescribed Officer' to avoid misuse

conditions: if presented for payment again at any of the bank branches shall be rejected under Rule 6(2) of NRR
requirements.full_sentence: (b) Mutilated / imperfect notes bearing 'PAY'/'PAID' (or 'REJECT') stamp of any RBI Issue Office or any bank branch, if presented for payment again at any of the bank branches shall be rejected under Rule 6(2) of NRR, 2009 and the tenderer shall be advised that the value of such note/s cannot be paid since the same has already been paid as is evident from the PAY/ PAID stamps affixed on it/ them.
requirements.object: 6(2) of NRR, 2009 and the tenderer shall be advised that the value of such note/s cannot be paid since the same has already bee

requirements.full_sentence: The branches shall caution their customers not to accept such notes from any bank or anybody else.
requirements.object: to accept such notes from any bank or anybody else

requirements.full_sentence: (b) Such notes received from members of public shall not be reissued for circulation and be remitted to currency chest for onward remittance to RBI offices.
requirements.object: and be remitted to currency chest for onward remittance to RBI offices

requirements.full_sentence: Similarly, notes which are disfigured may also be rejected under Rule 6(3) (ii) of NRR, 2009 (Amendment Rules, 2018).
requirements.object: Rule 6(3) (ii) of NRR, 2009 (Amendment Rules, 2018)

conditions: if presented for payment of exchange value shall be rejected under Rule 6(3) (ii) of the NRR
requirements.full_sentence:
Deliberately Cut Notes
The notes which are found to be deliberately cut, torn, altered or tampered with, if presented for payment of exchange value shall be rejected under Rule 6(3) (ii) of the NRR, 2009.
requirements.object: 6(3) (ii) of the NRR, 2009

requirements.full_sentence: The details of such instances together with the name of the tenderer, the number of notes tendered and their denominations, shall be reported to the DGM/ GM, Issue Department, Reserve Bank of India under whose jurisdiction the branch falls.
requirements.object: DGM/ GM, Issue Department, Reserve Bank of India under whose jurisdiction the branch falls

requirements.full_sentence: The matter shall also be reported to local police in case a large number of such notes are tendered.
requirements.object: local police in case a large number of such notes are tendered

requirements.full_sentence: The half value paid notes and rejected notes, which are held by the chest branches in their cash balance, shall be packed separately with separate invoice and sent along with the full value paid notes as part of soiled remittance or sent by registered and insured post as and when required.
requirements.object: separate invoice and sent along with the full value paid notes as part of soiled remittance or sent by registered and insure

requirements.full_sentence: All chest branches are required to submit to our RBI Issue Offices a monthly statement showing the number of notes adjudicated during the month.
requirements.object: RBI Issue Offices a monthly statement showing the number of notes adjudicated during the month

requirements.full_sentence:
Display of Notice Board
All bank branches are required to display at their branch premises, at a prominent place, a board indicating the availability of note and coin exchange facility with the legend, "SOILED/ MUTILATED NOTES AND COINS ARE ACCEPTED AND EXCHANGED HERE" for information of general public.
requirements.object: branch premises, at a prominent place, a board indicating the availability of note and coin exchange facility with the legend, "SO

requirements.full_sentence: (a) The Regional Managers / Zonal Managers of the banks shall pay surprise visits to the branches and report the position of compliance in this regard to the Head Office which will review such reports and take prompt remedial action, wherever necessary.
requirements.object: the branches and report the position of compliance in this regard to the Head Office which will review such reports and take prompt remedial action

requirements.full_sentence: (b) Any non-compliance in this regard shall be viewed as violation of instructions issued by the Reserve Bank of India and penalty, as applicable in terms of Master Direction on Scheme of Penalties for bank branches and currency chests for deficiency in rendering customer service to the members of public, shall be imposed.
requirements.object: of instructions issued by the Reserve Bank of India and penalty, as applicable in terms of Master Direction on Scheme of Penalt
